domain: decorative arts (cuir de Cordoue / Cordovan: 16-17th century Iberian gilded-and-embossed leather wall hangings)
specific_mechanism: Cordovan production: wet leather shaped over wooden moulds, painted, oil-gilded, lacquered; stamped/pressed with brass tools bearing motif wheels and handles to produce repeated relief patterns. The substrate (leather) preserves the impressed texture under repeated stamping cycles; lacquering then locks the pattern. Each tool produces a specific motif; the overall design is the composition of tool-stamps applied in spatial sequence on the substrate.
llm_application: Texture-tool decoder: at output time, deploy a small set of 'stamping' modules each producing a learned local stylistic motif (formal register, technical-precision, narrative-cadence). Decoding cycles apply stamps in spatial sequence over a 'leather' canvas of base content, with lacquering = a final smoothing pass that locks the pattern. Feedback attenuation: applying the same stamp on the same region produces diminishing-marginal returns (the leather only takes the impression once), so the output naturally converges to a stable patterned form rather than oscillating between styles.
novelty_claim: StyleTex (2411.00399), StyleStudio (CVPR 2025), TexGarment, ZeroStylus dual-layered LLM style transfer, ScribbleSense — all address texture/style generation. None specifically maps to Cordovan-style impression-tool repertoire applied to a substrate canvas with saturating-impression feedback attenuation.
